_comment: fleetdash config TEMPLATE — copy to ~/.cyberware/fleet.json and fill with YOUR nodes (that copy is YOURS and is never committed). One row per govd node. `url` = the node's TAILNET (overlay) address + the govd port (default 5773); govd binds the overlay IP, never a public interface. `role`: 'body' (delegated, exod-confined execution) | 'anchor' (cooperative, client-side execution). Monitor tokens come from `token_file` (a path to that node's minted monitor token) or env GOVD_MONITOR_TOKEN_<NAME> — NEVER inline. Stand each node up with deploy/fleet-setup.sh; it derives the host's overlay IP and prints the row to paste into your fleet.json. Do NOT put real node IPs/names in this template — those live only in your local ~/.cyberware/fleet.json. The optional per-node `arch`/`tier` annotations are surfaced by the :8773 fleet-discovery plane (`tier` = TRUST ceiling, core>verified>community). `fleet_tier` is the ORTHOGONAL topology HIERARCHY (mothership>edge>subagent>… — lower = higher authority); it groups the dashboard side-nav and is the axis cws-fleet:deploy contains against (a node may spawn only a STRICTLY-lower fleet_tier subagent). A node can be tier=core (high trust) AND fleet_tier=subagent (low in the hierarchy). The same roster feeds fleetd via env FLEETD_FLEET (a mounted file) or FLEETD_FLEET_URL (a remote provider).
